docs.title: Manthan — Decision Operating System
docs.text: Deterministic, auditable decision infrastructure for AI systems.

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
AI systems are non-deterministic.

  • Same input → different outputs
  • No traceability
  • No enforcement
  • No trust

docs.title: The Solution
docs.text:
Manthan introduces a deterministic decision layer.

  • Rule-based execution
  • Immutable contracts
  • Full audit logs
  • Enforceable outcomes

docs.title: System Overview
docs.text: Input ↓ Canonicalization ↓ Decision Engine ↓ Intelligence Layer ↓ Enforcement

docs.title: Key Capabilities
docs.text:
• Deterministic decision engine
  • Decision intelligence (score, priority, explanation)
  • Decision graphs (dependency execution)
  • Contract-based evolution
  • GitHub PR enforcement

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
```json { "risk": 8, "amount": 12000 } Decision { "decision": "REJECT", "score": 85, "priority": "HIGH" } Why It Matters

Without Manthan:

AI = unpredictable system

With Manthan:

AI = controlled, auditable infrastructure Links GitHub: https://github.com/pavancharak

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
# API Reference

Manthan exposes a deterministic decision endpoint.

---

## Endpoint

POST /decide

---

## Request

### Headers

``` id="3s3md7"

Content-Type: application/json

Body

{

"risk": 8,

"amount": 12000,

"type": "loan"

}

Processing Pipeline

Input → Canonicalization → Decision Engine → Intelligence → Output

``` id="3i9f2k"

---

## Response

```json

{

"decision": "REJECT",

"score": 85,

"confidence": 0.85,

"priority": "HIGH"

}

Deterministic Guarantees

Same input → same output

No randomness

No time dependency

Rule order is fixed

Error Handling

Invalid Input

{

"error": "INVALID_SCHEMA"

}

Unsupported Fields

{

"error": "UNSUPPORTED_FIELD"

}

Example Request

curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/decide \

-H "Content-Type: application/json" \

-d '{

"risk": 8,

"amount": 12000,

"type": "loan"

}'

Example Response

{

"decision": "REJECT",

"score": 85,

"confidence": 0.85,

"priority": "HIGH"

}

Contract Versioning

Every decision is tied to a version:

{

"version": "v1"

}

Notes

Stateless execution

Idempotent requests

Fully auditable outputs

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
# Architecture

Manthan is a **deterministic decision infrastructure** built as layered components.

---

## System Flow

Input → Canonicalization → Decision Engine → Intelligence → Enforcement

---

## Components

### Canonicalization

**Purpose:** Normalize input into a stable format

- Sort keys

- Normalize values

- Remove ambiguity

**Output:** Stable hash

---

### Decision Engine

**Purpose:** Core decision computation

- Rule-based

- Fixed execution order

- First-match wins

- No randomness

---

### Intelligence Layer

**Purpose:** Add structured metadata

- Score

- Confidence

- Priority

- Explanation

---

### Decision Graphs

**Purpose:** Handle dependencies

- DAG only

- Topological execution

- No cycles

---

### Contracts

**Purpose:** Define decision behavior

- Versioned

- Immutable

- Auditable

---

### Enforcement

**Purpose:** Apply decisions externally

- GitHub PR blocking

- API enforcement

- Workflow control

---

## System Guarantee

**Same Input → Same Output → Always**

---

## Design Principles

- Determinism over intelligence

- Explicit over implicit

- Versioned over mutable

- Auditability over performance

docs.title: Example Walkthrough
docs.text:
# Example Walkthrough

This example shows how a decision flows through Manthan.

---

## Step 1: Input

```json

{

"risk": 8,

"amount": 12000,

"type": "loan"

}

Step 2: Canonicalization

Transform input into deterministic format:

{

"amount": 12000,

"risk": 8,

"type": "loan"

}

Keys sorted

Values normalized

Step 3: Decision Engine

Rules evaluated in fixed order:

Rule 1: risk > 7 → REJECT

Rule 2: amount > 10000 → REVIEW

Rule 3: default → APPROVE

Evaluation:

risk = 8 → matches Rule 1

Step 4: Decision Output

{

"decision": "REJECT"

}

Step 5: Intelligence Layer

Enhance decision:

{

"score": 85,

"confidence": 0.85,

"priority": "HIGH"

}

Step 6: Final Output

{

"decision": "REJECT",

"score": 85,

"confidence": 0.85,

"priority": "HIGH"

}

Step 7: Enforcement

Example:

GitHub PR is blocked

Workflow is stopped

Full Flow

Guarantee

Same input → Same output → Always
